data.title: Bny mellon outlines 5% revenue growth target and raises medium-term margin goals amid AI-driven transformation
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: The Bank of New York Mellon Corporation (BK) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Robin Vince, President, CEO & Chairman of the Board, reported, "we delivered record net income of $5.3 billion on record revenue of $20.1 billion and generated a return on tangible common equity of 26%." He highlighted the company's achievement of "4 consecutive quarters of positive operating leverage in 2025, bringing us to 8 consecutive quarters overall." Vince emphasized strategic progress, including a new commercial model, platform transition with 70% of staff now in the new operating model, and advances in AI through the Eliza platform, supported by collaborations with Google Cloud and OpenAI. He called attention to product innovation such as the Dreyfus Stablecoin Reserves Fund and a step toward deposit tokenization on BNY's digital assets platform. Vince stated, "the next phase of our journey to unlock BNY's potential is about realizing scale and growth opportunities across our company."
  • Dermot McDonogh, Senior Executive VP & CFO, stated, "Total revenue of $5.2 billion was up 7% year-over-year. Fee revenue was up 5%. This included 8% growth in investment services fees, primarily driven by net new business, higher market values and higher client activity." McDonogh reported that "expenses of $3.4 billion were flat year-over-year on a reported basis and up 4% excluding notable items," and detailed significant growth in net interest income, assets under custody/administration, and assets under management.

Outlook

  • McDonogh shared, "the guidance that we're giving to you for 2026 is up 5% plus or minus year-on-year on the top line revenue." He clarified, "NII for us this year is a little bit ahead of 5%, fees may be a little bit lower than 5%. So all in, top line growth up 5%." Expense growth for 2026 is expected at "approximately 3% to 4% growth in expenses, excluding notable items." For the medium term, McDonogh announced, "we are increasing our pretax margin target by 500 basis points to 38%, and we are increasing our return on tangible common equity target also by 500 basis points to 28%." The company maintains a Tier 1 leverage ratio management target of 5.5% to 6%.

Financial Results

  • The company reported total revenue of $5.2 billion for the quarter, with fee revenue up 5% and net interest income up 13% year-over-year. Earnings per share reached $2.02, up 31% year-over-year, and excluding notable items, $2.08, up 21%. Total revenue for the full year was $20.1 billion, with fee revenue up 6%. Pretax margin was 36% on a reported basis and 37% excluding notable items for the quarter. Return on tangible common equity was 27% for the quarter and 26% for the year. Asset servicing, corporate trust, and collateral management each saw double-digit fee growth, and the company reported that "ETF AUC/A of $3.8 trillion ended the year up 34% year-over-year." Security Services segment pretax margin reached 34% for the quarter and 33% for the year. Market and Wealth Services segment reported pretax income of $882 million and a pretax margin of 49%.

Q&A

  • Ebrahim Poonawala, BofA Securities, Research Division, asked about the assumptions underpinning the 2026 revenue growth outlook. McDonogh explained, "Q4 is a good jumping off point, but December was a particularly strong month for us in NII...NII for us this year is a little bit ahead of 5%, fees may be a little bit lower than 5%. So all in, top line growth up 5%."
  • Michael Mayo, Wells Fargo Securities, LLC, Research Division, inquired about AI's impact on hiring and efficiency. Vince responded, "Making AI for everyone, everywhere and for everything at BNY is our mantra...we are short-term enthusiastic, medium-term excited and long term, believing that it will have a significant positive impact." McDonogh added, "AI is unlocking capacity. We don't think about it in the narrow definition of efficiency. It's all about growing with clients, increasing revenues and optimizing the potential for our employees."
  • Kenneth Usdin, Bernstein Autonomous LLP, requested detail on pretax margin improvement. McDonogh stated, "We have 3 segments: Securities Services; we see upside in Corporate Trust; we see upside in Depository Receipts...Market and Wealth Services is the most akin to platforms at scale...the upside from there on that segment was probably a little bit muted. But in Investment and Wealth Management...that's where we see the most opportunity in '26 and beyond."
  • Steven Chubak, Wolfe Research, LLC, questioned demand for tokenized deposit capabilities. Vince replied, "We see global financial markets as transforming, moving towards more of an always-on operating model...our road map has really been, right from the beginning, focus on the innovation, be able to bring the capabilities online."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts pressed for clarity on revenue composition, sustainability of organic growth, and tangible AI savings, showing a neutral to slightly skeptical tone, particularly about the pace of fee growth and the expense outlook.
  • Management maintained a confident and constructive tone, frequently citing "confidence" and "momentum" and highlighting progress and future ambitions. During Q&A, management showed openness but sometimes leaned on phrases such as "we think" and "we expect," indicating careful optimism.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone was more assertive in setting higher medium-term targets, while analysts were more persistent in probing for underlying growth drivers and risks.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted from segment-specific to total revenue guidance for 2026, targeting 5% growth, compared to the previous quarter's segmental outlook.
  • Medium-term targets for pretax margin and return on tangible common equity increased by 500 basis points each.
  • Management highlighted continued positive operating leverage and the transition to a higher-growth phase, versus last quarter's focus on foundational transformation.
  • Analysts' questions shifted to focus more on AI, digital assets, and the sustainability of growth, with increased scrutiny of the organic vs. market-driven revenue mix.
  • The prior quarter emphasized the maturing of the commercial and platform models; this quarter, the focus was on scale, efficiency, and unlocking further margin improvements.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited a "very dynamic operating environment" and emphasized agility in the expense base to respond to potential revenue shortfalls.
  • Vince noted, "we've also deliberately positioned the platforms inside the company and the whole company to trying to reduce the macro sensitivity to the world."
  • McDonogh highlighted that "we will continue to manage ourselves conservatively to the upper end" of the Tier 1 leverage ratio range.
  • Analysts voiced concerns over the sustainability of organic growth, the potential for fee revenue to lag overall revenue, and questioned whether recent strong NII performance is repeatable.

Final Takeaway

The Bank of New York Mellon's management conveyed strong momentum entering 2026, underpinned by record financial performance, enhanced operating leverage, and ambitious new medium-term financial targets. The company emphasized a strategic pivot towards realizing scale and growth, leveraging investments in AI, digital assets, and an integrated commercial model. Management remains confident in its ability to deliver resilient growth, maintain expense discipline, and capitalize on both traditional and emerging financial market trends, while remaining agile to address risks in a dynamic operating landscape.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Euromax announces repayment of debt
data.description:
• Euromax Resources (EOX:CA) on Tuesday stated that it has entered into an agreement dated Jan. 13, 2026, to fully settle outstanding debt owed to major shareholder Galena Resource Equities.
  • The debt, totaling $0.80M, was owed to Galena, controlled by Galena Asset Management, an affiliate of Trafigura, under a non-interest-bearing, unsecured, non-convertible promissory note issued on Sept. 29, 2025.
  • The company will settle the $0.80M debt by issuing 34.97M common shares to Galena at a deemed price of C$0.0325 (US$0.02299) per share.
  • Pursuant to the transaction, Galena would beneficially own 555.55M common shares, representing 57.97% of Euromax’s issued and outstanding shares, an increase of 1.59%.
  • Closing of the transaction is subject to customary closing conditions.

data.title: Reflex Advanced Materials announces shares for debt settlement
data.description:
• Reflex Advanced Materials (RFLXF) said on Tuesday it has entered into a debt settlement agreement with a service provider of the company.
  • Under the agreement, the company will settle $45,000 of debt through the issuance of 246,981 common shares at a deemed price of $0.1822 per share.
  • The agreement and related share issuance are subject to CSE approval, with the securities subject to a four-month-and-one-day hold period.
  • Shares +7.38%.

data.title: Musk says AI will lead to universal abundance and saving for retirement will be irrelevant
data.description:
Billionaire businessman Elon Musk last week called artificial intelligence and robotics a "supersonic tsunami" that will lead to abundance for all humans, making saving for retirement irrelevant.

Musk's remarks came in an appearance on Peter Diamandis' podcast on YouTube last Tuesday. He has long been an advocate of AI and robotics and has made several public comments supporting the technology. On the podcast, Musk said he is "more optimistic than most people" about the effects of AI.

"Don't worry about squirreling money away for retirement in 10 or 20 years. It won't matter," Musk said. He believes AI will lead to people having a "universal you-can-have-whatever-you-want income." 

"My best guess for how this will manifest is that prices will...drop. So as the efficiency of production or the provision of services drops, prices will drop," the billionaire said. 

"I mean prices in dollar terms are the ratio between the output of goods and services and the money supply. So if your output of goods and services increases faster than the money supply, you will have deflation, or vice versa," he added.

Musk also painted a picture of a future where AI would have outstripped human intellect and people would be outnumbered by robots.

"There's going to be more intelligence, digital intelligence, than all human intelligence combined and more humanoid robots than all humans," he said.

"The white-collar laborer will be the first to go because until you can move atoms, the thing that can be replaced first is anything that involves just digital," Musk noted.

"Even with AI at its current state, I'd say you're pretty close to being able to replace half of all jobs, of white-collar jobs; that includes anything like education, too. Anything that involves information. And anything short of shaping atoms, AI can do probably half or more of those jobs right now," the businessman added.

Musk, the world's richest person, is the CEO of electric vehicle maker Tesla (TSLA), space exploration and aerospace firm SpaceX (SPACE), founder of startup xAI (X.AI) through which he owns X (formerly Twitter), and leader of Neuralink and The Boring Company.

Seeking Alpha's trending AI stocks: (PATH), (DELL), (AMBA), (AMD), (BBAI), (ADSK), (III), (TWLO), (ANET), (KVYO), (FRSH), (APP), (INTA), (OS), (API), and (WDH).

Here are some AI- and robotics-focused exchange-traded funds: (AIQ), (BOTZ), (DTEC), (WTAI), (XAIX), (WISE), (GINN), (ROBT), (TECB), (XT), (THNQ), and (CHAT).

data.title: AirJoule Technologies announces proposed public offering of class A common stock
data.description:
• AirJoule Technologies (AIRJ) on Tuesday announced the commencement of an underwritten public offering of shares of its Class A common stock, par value $0.0001 per share.
  • The company said Patrick Eilers, Executive Chairman and member of Board of Directors, CEO Matthew Jore, and Stuart Porter, a member of Board of Directors, have indicated an interest in purchasing shares of Class A common stock in this offering at the public offering price per share and on the same terms as the other purchasers in this offering. 
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds it receives from the offering to fund growth capital, working capital and for general corporate purposes.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Mid to low cap materials stocks with the strongest earnings momentum
data.description:
As the earnings season kicks off, we look at mid-to-low market cap materials stocks with strong earnings momentum, which often attract investor attention as positive analyst revisions to earnings estimates can signal improving fundamentals.

The EPS Revision rating evaluates how analysts have adjusted their earnings per share estimates for a company over recent periods, with upgrades suggesting growing confidence in a company’s profitability outlook.

The following low to mid cap materials sector stocks have earned the highest possible EPS revision rating of A+, indicating the most favorable trajectory of analyst earnings estimate changes.

The following materials stocks have earned an A+ EPS revision grade:

  1. Constellium SE (CSTM) — EPS Revision Grade: A+
  2. Flotek Industries (FTK) — EPS Revision Grade: A+
  3. Fuchs SE (FUPBY) — EPS Revision Grade: A+
  4. Warrior Met Coal (HCC) — EPS Revision Grade: A+
  5. K+S Aktiengesellschaft (KPLUY) — EPS Revision Grade: A+
  6. Nexa Resources S.A. (NEXA) — EPS Revision Grade: A+

  8. Santacruz Silver Mining (SCZMF) — EPS Revision Grade: A+
  9. Stora Enso Oyj (SEOAY) — EPS Revision Grade: A+
  10. SunCoke Energy (SXC) — EPS Revision Grade: A+

 Materials ETFs: (XLB), (VAW), (IYM), (FXZ), (MXI), (RSPM)

data.title: Copa Holdings reports 10.9% rise in December passenger traffic
data.description:
• Copa Holdings (CPA) on Tuesday said its system-wide passenger traffic, measured in revenue passenger miles (RPMs), rose 10.9% in December 2025 from a year earlier.
  • Capacity, measured in available seat miles (ASMs), increased 10.0% during the month, while the load factor improved to 86.0% from 85.3% in December 2024.
  • CPA -0.0% after hours to $130.05.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Guardian Pharmacy expects 2026 revenue between $1.40B and $1.42B
data.description:
• Guardian Pharmacy Services (GRDN) on Tuesday said it expects revenue in the range of $1.40 billion to $1.42 billion for 2026, after the effects of the drug-pricing reforms under the Inflation Reduction Act and the more recently announced wholesale acquisition cost reductions.
  • Excluding the impact of the mandatory drug price reductions, the company would have projected 2026 revenue growth in the high single digits.
  • The company said it expects to continue its low double-digit adjusted EBITDA growth trajectory in 2026, supported by the strength of its operating model and increased visibility into the year ahead, including anticipated drug pricing changes.
  • The company expects 2026 adjusted EBITDA in the range of $115 million to $118 million, representing approximately 11% year-over-year growth from 2025 guidance.
  • Guardian is reiterating its previously announced 2025 revenue guidance of $1.43 billion to $1.45 billion and adjusted EBITDA guidance of $104 million to $106 million. 
  • GRDN +1.98% after hours to $31.88.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Taseko completes Florence construction, reports higher H2 Gibraltar copper output
data.description:
Taseko Mines (TGB) closed +3.9% on Tuesday, reaching a multiyear high of $6.56, after saying  construction activity at its Florence copper project in Arizona is complete, with its focus now turning to wellfield operations and commissioning of the SX/EW plant.

Injection flowrates in the wellfield have met or exceeded expectations to-date, resulting in faster initial acidification of the wellfield, Taseko (TGB) said, and the grade of copper recovered in solution from the recovery wells has continued to increase, with the average solution grade reaching the level required for SX/EW plant operations, which are expected to begin soon.

At the Gibraltar mine in British Columbia, Taseko (TGB) reported 2025 copper production of 98M lbs and molybdenum production of 1.9M lbs, with full-year sales of copper and molybdenum totaliing 99M lbs and 1.9M lbs, respectively.

Q4 Gibraltar copper production totaled 31M lbs, a significant increase over previous quarters of 2025, with copper head grade increasing to 0.26%, in line with expectations, while molybdenum output in the quarter was 800K lbs, ~50% higher than Q3 and the best quarterly production in eight years.

"Gibraltar production in the second half of the year was a notable improvement over the first half of the year with higher grades and better quality ore. Looking ahead to 2026, we expect more consistent quarterly production, now that we are better situated in the Connector pit, and higher overall copper production," President and CEO Stuart McDonald said.
